gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) remains in scaffold state with all Articles I–VI marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The organism's constitutional identity, scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, and interpretation rules are undefined.
gaps.mission_ref: BoR Articles I, II, III, IV, V, VI; MSP Relationship to the Bill of Rights (MSP strictly subordinate, any MSP rule that conflicts with BoR is void)

gaps.description: The Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) contains zero operational rules (bootstrap seed). Nomos (#200) evaluates Action Proposals against MSP + genome, but only a default-permissive evaluation is active. The MSP does not yet translate any BoR provisions into machine-actionable objectives, invariants, constraints, or safety rules.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP Purpose, MSP Rules section; MSP Amendment Protocol (no PEMs have triggered rule creation)

gaps.description: Currently dispatched goals (16 goals, mixed priorities) all contain mission_ref placeholders like 'MSP §critical-priority', 'MSP §high-priority', etc. The MSP contains no such sections. Goals are not derived from any substantive mission statement and may be defaults or test artifacts.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP Purpose ('translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules')

gaps.description: No genome documents exist under genome/ to extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules or risk-tier-specific constraints.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP Genome References; MSP Purpose (machine-actionable constraints for Nomos evaluation)

gaps.description: The MSP amendment pipeline (PEM → Senate-Legislator review → human approval → Rollout) has never triggered. No policy exception messages have been generated because no substantive MSP rules exist to block APs, leaving the amendment machinery untested.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Absence of Organ Availability SLAs and Stability Baselines",
      "description": "The MSP Rules section is empty, providing no organ availability targets, degradation-duration tolerances, or recovery-time objectives. The Spine-State Slice shows all 27 organs rapidly oscillating between ALIVE and DEGRADED in ~30-second cycles (e.g., Receptor flips 8 times in 5 minutes), yet no policy governs acceptable state-churn, flapping thresholds, or required dampening. This absence leaves the DIO with no operational definition of 'healthy' vs. 'degraded' service, making it impossible to determine when manual intervention is required.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP §Rules (empty bootstrap seed, 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules')",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 9
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "Undefined Priority-Tier Operational Rules",
      "description": "Recently dispatched goals reference mission citations like 'MSP §critical-priority', 'MSP §high-priority', 'MSP §medium-priority', and 'MSP §low-priority', but no such sections exist in the current MSP. Without defined priority tiers, there are no operational constraints on goal ordering, resource allocation, preemption, or starvation prevention. This creates a risk of mission-critical work being delayed by lower-priority tasks, and leaves Nomos without rule-based adjudication guidance.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP §Rules (empty) — referenced priority sections are undefined",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 8
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Missing Runtime Cadence and Latency Baselines",
      "description": "The MSP contains no target cadence for goal processing, no maximum end-to-end latency expectations, and no throughput targets. The Spine transitions show recovery from DEGRADED to ALIVE occurs within milliseconds of a missed pong (e.g., all organs transition back simultaneously), indicating rapid detection but no governed backoff or scheduling policy. Without runtime baselines, there is no way to measure performance degradation of the goal-execution pipeline or detect when the system is falling behind its operational tempo.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP §Rules (empty — no runtime performance expectations defined)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": 6
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "No Operational Alerting or Escalation Criteria for State Flapping",
      "description": "The Spine transitions document repeated 'missed_pong' → 'pong_recovered' cycles for every organ, suggesting a systemic health-signal instability that is not addressed by any MSP rule. There is no defined threshold for generating Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) on excessive state transitions, no escalation path to human operators, and no requirement for Spine or Minder to dampen or log anomalous patterns. This gap means the system could enter a thrashing failure mode without any internal process flagging it as an operational incident.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP §Amendment Protocol (only mentions PEM for policy blockage, not operational events) and empty Rules",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": 6
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "Constitutional Void — Bill of Rights Scaffold Empty",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is a scaffold with all articles marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. There is no defined scope of enterprise, no list of prohibited activities, no human authority reservation, and no amendment protocol. Arbiter (#190) verifies the BoR at every scope query, but the document contains no constraints, effectively granting unlimited scope to all action proposals. This is the root cause of missing operational boundaries; without a BoR, the MSP cannot safely define enforceable rules.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Articles I–VI (all scaffolds, awaiting human authorship)",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "critical",
      "priority_score": 10
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-006",
      "title": "No Organizational Rollback or Degradation-Dampening Rules",
      "description": "The Spine data shows instant mass-recovery: as soon as a single pong succeeds, all DEGRADED organs transition to ALIVE in a tight burst (e.g., all 27 within ~0.3s at 14:49:13). There is no progressive recovery, backoff logic, or per-organ health-gating defined in the MSP. This can lead to cascading re-degradations if the underlying issue is transient but persistent. The MSP should define stabilization windows and incremental recovery states to prevent thrash-loops.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP §Rules (empty — no policy for state-transition governance)",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": 6
    }
  ]
}

{

      "id": "gap-bor-article-I-scope",
      "title": "Undefined Scope of Enterprise leaves person-attention boundaries unenforced",
      "description": "Minder observations extensively document the four IP-protected projects (Graphheight, Opvera, Sentience, nnetcast) and their supporting systems. Without a defined Scope of Enterprise, the DIO cannot distinguish authorized enterprise activities from mission creep, risking actions outside Leon's intended project boundaries.",
